title: boechera consanguinea
intro: Boechera consanguinea, also known as Congdon's rockcress, is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is used medicinally for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant's medicinal properties are believed to promote health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and supporting the immune system. Boechera consanguinea can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote wound healing and skin health.
